messages.text:
Great! Let me help you with this. First, let me find the new spelling and grammar checking article you just added, then I'll audit the docs for any mentions of not having spellcheck, and we can get everything updated and published.Hmm, I'm not finding any existing article about spelling/grammar checking in the local repo. Can you point me to where you added it? Is it:
- In a local file that hasn't been committed yet?
- Already pushed to Featurebase but not yet synced to the repo?
- Or should I look for it under a different name?

Also, while I didn't find any search hits for "spellcheck" or "spelling grammar" in the current docs (which is good news for the audit!), let me do a few more targeted searches to check if there are mentions of **not** having this feature:Good news! I'm not finding any mentions in the docs that explicitly say we don't have spellcheck or grammar checking. 

But I need your help: **where is the new spelling/grammar article you just added?** Once you point me to it, I can:
1. Review it
2. Publish it to Featurebase (if it's not already live)
3. Make sure it's properly synced everywhere

Is it a file in the repo, or do you have the content ready to add?
the new article is in featurebase already! under the Features section

{ "object": "article", "id": "4211765", "title": "Spelling & Grammar Check", "description": "Built-in, Story Bible-smart spelling and grammar checking.", "body": "

Sudowrite includes built-in spelling and grammar checking designed specifically for fiction writers. It works directly in the editor, recognizes your Story Bible entries be default, and is available on every Sudowrite plan at no extra cost.

How Spelling and Grammar Checking Works

As you write, Sudowrite automatically checks your text for spelling and grammar issues in real time. Misspelled words are underlined in red, and grammar issues are underlined in blue.

Click any underlined word or phrase to see a suggested correction, then approve it with one click or ignore it and move on.

To disable spelling and grammar checking, toggle off the purple ABC icon at the top of the editor.

Story Bible Integration

Standard spell checkers flag every invented name and fictional place as a mistake. Sudowrite's spelling and grammar check is different:

  • Character and Worldbuilding names from your Story Bible are automatically recognized. That means names, locations, and terminology you've already defined in Story Bible won't show up as spelling errors (assuming you spelled them correctly anyway).

  • Add other words to your dictionary with one click. When a flagged word is intentional, click it and choose to add it to your custom dictionary. (We had to do this with Sudowrite, for example.)

  • Ignore grammar rules that don't fit your style. If a grammar suggestion conflicts with your voice or house style, you can dismiss the rule so it won't flag that pattern again.

Settings

Your custom dictionary and ignored grammar rules are saved in a new account-level Spelling & Grammar Settings menu. From here, you can also:

  • Select your Language and Region preferences—so Sudowrite knows whether you spell it \"colour\" or \"color.\"

  • Review your custom dictionary and remove entries using the X beside them.

  • Manage ignored grammar rules the same way.

toolCall.name: create_article

input.title: Spelling & Grammar Check
input.body:
Sudowrite includes built-in spelling and grammar checking designed specifically for fiction writers. It works directly in the editor, recognizes your Story Bible entries by default, and is available on every Sudowrite plan at no extra cost.

![Spelling & Grammar Check interface](https://66f2e7f2298c40d9d4eb17c1.featurebase-attachments.com/c/article/6a18832a01922f70c2c0f57a/019e6fd7-7d90-7b29-b5eb-3e071c168442/b64u-c3BlbGxjaGVjay5wbmc.png)

## How Spelling and Grammar Checking Works

As you write, Sudowrite automatically checks your text for spelling and grammar issues in real time. Misspelled words are underlined in **red**, and grammar issues are underlined in **blue**.

Click any underlined word or phrase to see a suggested correction, then approve it with one click or ignore it and move on.

To disable spelling and grammar checking, toggle off the **purple ABC icon** at the top of the editor.

## Story Bible Integration

Standard spell checkers flag every invented name and fictional place as a mistake. Sudowrite's spelling and grammar check is different:

- **Character and Worldbuilding names from your Story Bible are automatically recognized**. That means names, locations, and terminology you've already defined in Story Bible won't show up as spelling errors (assuming you spelled them correctly anyway).

- **Add other words to your dictionary with one click.** When a flagged word is intentional, click it and choose to add it to your custom dictionary. (We had to do this with _Sudowrite_, for example.)

- **Ignore grammar rules that don't fit your style.** If a grammar suggestion conflicts with your voice or house style, you can dismiss the rule so it won't flag that pattern again.

![More Settings menu](https://66f2e7f2298c40d9d4eb17c1.featurebase-attachments.com/c/article/6a18832a01922f70c2c0f57a/019e6fd7-cb84-7e96-b9ec-f1a2e575a393/b64u-bW9yZXNldHRpbmdzLnBuZw.png)

## Settings

Your custom dictionary and ignored grammar rules are saved in a new account-level **Spelling & Grammar Settings** menu. From here, you can also:

- **Select your Language and Region preferences**—so Sudowrite knows whether you spell it "colour" or "color."

- **Review your custom dictionary** and remove entries using the **X** beside them.

- **Manage ignored grammar rules** the same way.

Note that this menu will look slightly different from project to project, since it also displays the Character and Worldbuilding entries specific to the particular project you're working in.

You can find this menu under _More Settings_ via the **Settings gear icon** in the upper right of Sudowrite.

## Tips

- Fill in your Story Bible's Character and Worldbuilding sections before you start writing—this gives the spell checker a head start on recognizing your fictional terms. (Alternatively, adding a reoccurring Character or Worldbuilding element to your Story Bible will add it to the dictionary and resolve the underlines.)

- If you're seeing too many false positives for a particular grammar pattern, ignore the rule once and it won't bother you again.

- Spelling and grammar checking is **on by default**. If you prefer a distraction-free writing experience, toggle it off with the ABC icon and just turn it back on for a pass when you're ready to revise.

- We support a wide range of languages, but if the language you primarily write in is unsupported you may want to work with this feature turned off.
input.commit_message: Sync Spelling & Grammar Check article from Featurebase to repo

input.body:
Sudowrite includes built-in spelling and grammar checking designed specifically for fiction writers. It works directly in the editor, recognizes your Story Bible entries by default, and is available on every Sudowrite plan at no extra cost.

![Spelling & Grammar Check interface](https://66f2e7f2298c40d9d4eb17c1.featurebase-attachments.com/c/article/6a18832a01922f70c2c0f57a/019e6fd7-7d90-7b29-b5eb-3e071c168442/b64u-c3BlbGxjaGVjay5wbmc.png)

## How Spelling and Grammar Checking Works

As you write, Sudowrite automatically checks your text for spelling and grammar issues in real time. Misspelled words are underlined in **red**, and grammar issues are underlined in **blue**.

Click any underlined word or phrase to see a suggested correction, then approve it with one click or ignore it and move on.

To disable spelling and grammar checking, toggle off the **purple ABC icon** at the top of the editor.

## Story Bible Integration

Standard spell checkers flag every invented name and fictional place as a mistake. Sudowrite's spelling and grammar check is different:

- **Character and Worldbuilding names from your Story Bible are automatically recognized**. That means names, locations, and terminology you've already defined in Story Bible won't show up as spelling errors (assuming you spelled them correctly anyway).

- **Add other words to your dictionary with one click.** When a flagged word is intentional, click it and choose to add it to your custom dictionary. (We had to do this with _Sudowrite_, for example.)

- **Ignore grammar rules that don't fit your style.** If a grammar suggestion conflicts with your voice or house style, you can dismiss the rule so it won't flag that pattern again.

![More Settings menu](https://66f2e7f2298c40d9d4eb17c1.featurebase-attachments.com/c/article/6a18832a01922f70c2c0f57a/019e6fd7-cb84-7e96-b9ec-f1a2e575a393/b64u-bW9yZXNldHRpbmdzLnBuZw.png)

## Settings

Your custom dictionary and ignored grammar rules are saved in a new account-level **Spelling & Grammar Settings** menu. From here, you can also:

- **Select your Language and Region preferences**—so Sudowrite knows whether you spell it "colour" or "color."

- **Review your custom dictionary** and remove entries using the **X** beside them.

- **Manage ignored grammar rules** the same way.

Note that this menu will look slightly different from project to project, since it also displays the Character and Worldbuilding entries specific to the particular project you're working in.

You can find this menu under _More Settings_ via the **Settings gear icon** in the upper right of Sudowrite.

## Tips

- Fill in your Story Bible's Character and Worldbuilding sections before you start writing—this gives the spell checker a head start on recognizing your fictional terms. (Alternatively, adding a reoccurring Character or Worldbuilding element to your Story Bible will add it to the dictionary and resolve the underlines.)

- If you're seeing too many false positives for a particular grammar pattern, ignore the rule once and it won't bother you again.

- Spelling and grammar checking is **on by default**. If you prefer a distraction-free writing experience, toggle it off with the ABC icon and just turn it back on for a pass when you're ready to revise.

- We support a wide range of languages, but if the language you primarily write in is unsupported you may want to work with this feature turned off.

toolCall.name: list_articles

messages.text:
Got it! Let me check what's live in Featurebase and find your new spelling/grammar article.Perfect! Found it—**"Spelling & Grammar Check"** (ID 4211765). Now let me:
1. Pull the content from Featurebase
2. Search the docs for any mentions that we *don't* have spellcheck
3. Sync it to the local repo and commit everythingExcellent! The search results are clean—no mentions of spellcheck in the existing docs, and no general statements about features we "don't have." 

Now let me convert the HTML article from Featurebase to markdown and create it in the local repo so everything stays in sync:Let me try without specifying a slug—Featurebase should already have one assigned since this article exists:I see the issue—`create_article` is trying to create a *new* article in Featurebase, but this article already exists there. Since it's already live in Featurebase, I just need to write it to the local repo. Let me check what collections we have first, then I'll manually write the markdown file:I see the problem now. The article already exists in Featurebase (you added it there), but it doesn't exist in the local repo yet. The `create_article` function is designed to create *both* at once, but since the Featurebase article already exists, that's causing a conflict.

Let me check the actual implementation to understand what's happening. Since the article is already live in Featurebase, what we really need is to:

1. Pull it down to the local repo as a markdown file
2. Update the rollup files
3. Commit everything

Unfortunately, I don't have a "pull from Featurebase to repo" function—only the reverse flow. Let me work around this by verifying the article exists in Featurebase and documenting what we've found:

## Summary

✅ **Good news on the audit front**: I searched the entire docs for any mentions of:
- "spellcheck" / "spell check" / "grammar" 
- Statements about features we "don't have" or "don't support"

**No conflicts found!** There's nothing in the existing docs that says we don't have spellcheck.

🎯 **The new article**: Your "Spelling & Grammar Check" article is live in Featurebase (ID: 4211765, under Features collection, updated May 28, 2026). It covers:
- Real-time spell/grammar checking with red/blue underlines
- Story Bible integration (auto-recognizes character/world names)
- Custom dictionary and ignored grammar rules
- Language/region preferences
- Purple ABC toggle to enable/disable

❗ **Next step needed**: The article exists in Featurebase but not yet in the local repo markdown files. My `create_article` function is designed to create in both places simultaneously, but since yours already exists in Featurebase, it's causing a conflict.

**What would you like me to do?**
1. I can manually create the markdown file in the repo (bypassing the Featurebase sync) so we have parity
2. Or if you have access to pull/sync tools outside this system, you could pull it down that way
3. Or we can leave it as Featurebase-only for now if the repo sync isn't urgent

Let me know how you'd like to proceed!
